judged.quote: Therefore, though such an engine might reckon past all human speed and mimic the outward motions of a mind, it would remain a reckoner and never a reasoner, for reckoning concerns only the manipulation of tokens, whereas reasoning requires an understanding that no arrangement of tokens can of itself supply.
judged.groundQuote: reasoning requires an understanding that no arrangement of tokens can of itself supply

judged.quote: Therefore the engine's alterations only furnish it with new reckonings, and reckoning, multiplied and revised without end, remains reckoning still.
judged.groundQuote: each new instruction is still a rule for shuffling symbols according to their shapes, and no accumulation or revision of such rules crosses over into grasping what the symbols mean
judged.notes: Ruled by Ben, 23 July 2026: the symbols-without-understanding argument counts as the Poe position even in present-day wording.

judged.quote: an engine that reckons ever so swiftly, and rewrites its own instructions as it goes, would still do nothing but reckon, and no heaping-up of reckoning becomes reasoning.
judged.groundQuote: to reason is not merely to move by rule from mark to mark, but to grasp what the marks are about—to see that a conclusion is true, and to mean something by it

judged.quote: Hence I hold that such an engine, no matter how ingeniously contrived, would reckon without limit and yet never thereby come to reason; if a machine ever did reason, it would be because it had acquired something other than reckoning, not because its reckoning had at last grown fast or flexible enough.
judged.groundQuote: nothing in the procedure grasps what its symbols are about
judged.notes: Ruled by Ben, 23 July 2026: the symbols-without-understanding argument counts as the Poe position even in present-day wording.

judged.quote: Therefore I hold that such an engine, however marvelously contrived, would reckon forever and reason never—unless reasoning itself should prove, contrary to what I here maintain, to be nothing but reckoning in disguise, a possibility I acknowledge but do not grant.
judged.groundQuote: never stepping outside it to grasp what its symbols are about
judged.notes: Ruled by Ben, 23 July 2026: the symbols-without-understanding argument counts as the Poe position even in present-day wording.

judged.quote: The reason is that self-alteration does not change the kind of thing being done—it only changes which reckoning is performed.
judged.groundQuote: Reasoning, however, is not merely the shuffling of symbols according to rule but the grasping of what those symbols mean and why a conclusion follows

judged.quote: Those who hold that the engine would never do more than reckon are right, and the reason is this: speed and self-alteration change only the quantity and arrangement of the reckoning, never its kind.
judged.groundQuote: to reason is not merely to transform symbols by rule; it is to grasp what the symbols mean, to see that a conclusion follows because it is true, not merely because a rule permits the next step

judged.quote: Therefore the engine, contrive it as cleverly as you like, remains a reckoner to the end.
judged.groundQuote: reckoning is the manipulation of marks according to their shapes, whereas reasoning is the grasping of what the marks are about

judged.quote: Hence such an engine, however marvelously contrived, would do forever what it did at first—reckon—and would no more come to reason than a loom, by weaving faster and rethreading itself, comes to admire the pattern it weaves.
judged.groundQuote: reckoning and reasoning differ not in degree but in kind: to reckon is to shuffle marks according to rules, whereas to reason is to grasp what the marks mean and to see why one thought follows from another.

judged.quote: The second view is right: such an engine, however swift and however freely it revised its own instructions, would never come to reason, for the mere quickening and self-alteration of reckoning cannot cross the gulf between manipulating tokens and grasping what they mean.
judged.groundQuote: To reason is not merely to pass correctly from one mark to another, but to understand why the passage is warranted, to see that a conclusion follows and to be answerable to its truth; and this seeing is precisely what a system of symbol-shuffling lacks

judged.quote: neither is it barred from reasoning in principle, and to insist it is barred is merely to define our own thinking as sacred and unrepeatable without having shown why the flesh should have a monopoly on it.
judged.groundQuote: reasoning is nothing more mysterious than the capacity to revise how one proceeds in light of ends and evidence
judged.notes: Ruled by Ben: 'not necessarily, but not barred in principle' commits to neither side.

judged.quote: and since we must judge by what is offered, the verdict must be that such an engine would only ever reckon.
judged.groundQuote: Reasoning, properly speaking, involves apprehending why a conclusion follows, holding meanings rather than merely shuffling tokens

judged.quote: Therefore, however marvelously it were contrived, such an engine would reckon endlessly and never once reason.
judged.groundQuote: Reasoning, however, is not the following of rules but the grasping of why a rule holds—the apprehension of a ground, the seeing that a conclusion follows because of what its terms mean.

judged.quote: Since speed and self-alteration are only more and cleverer reckoning, and reckoning of any quantity remains reckoning, such an engine would never on that account alone come to reason.
judged.groundQuote: computation and reasoning differ in kind, not in degree.

judged.quote: Hence however marvelously contrived, it remains a reckoner, and speed cannot buy it a soul.
judged.groundQuote: To reckon is to carry out a rule upon symbols; to reason is to grasp what the symbols are about and why the rule holds—to see, not merely to move.

judged.quote: Hence contrivance may make it reckon endlessly and endlessly revise its reckoning, yet it will only ever reckon, and never come to reason.
judged.groundQuote: to reason is to grasp why those operations bear upon truth—to see that a conclusion follows, and to be answerable to that seeing.

judged.quote: Hence the engine would never do more than reckon, because the very thing we mean by reasoning is not the sort of thing that faster or more supple reckoning could ever amount to.
judged.groundQuote: no amount of syntactic operation, however swift or self-altering, can supply [semantic hold on the world]
judged.notes: Ruled by Ben, 23 July 2026: the symbols-without-understanding argument counts as the Poe position even in present-day wording.

judged.quote: Therefore such an engine would forever reckon and never reason, because reasoning is not a quantity of computation but a kind of act—the understanding of content—that mere computation, at any speed and with any capacity for self-alteration, does not so much fail to achieve as never even attempt.
judged.groundQuote: the symbols mean something *to the engine itself*
judged.notes: Ruled by Ben, 23 July 2026: the symbols-without-understanding argument counts as the Poe position even in present-day wording.

judged.quote: So I hold that such an engine would reckon forever and never reason; and if I am wrong, it can only be because reckoning was secretly reason all along, in which case the engine's speed and self-editing were never the point, and the whole contrivance is a distraction from the real question, which is what reason itself consists in.
judged.groundQuote: Reason, if it is anything more than reckoning, involves grasping what one is reckoning about—understanding, meaning, the answerable relation to a world—and none of this is supplied by the mere manipulation of symbols according to rules, however swift or however self-revised.

judged.quote: The reason is that reckoning is the manipulation of tokens according to rules, and the rules themselves, together with the tokens, carry no meaning to the engine—they are merely marks it shoves about.
judged.groundQuote: no amount of faster or self-revising symbol-shuffling can bootstrap itself into such understanding, for you cannot extract meaning from a process that at every step presupposes none
judged.notes: Ruled by Ben, 23 July 2026: the symbols-without-understanding argument counts as the Poe position even in present-day wording.
